source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Riser Gantry Crane Drop

actions_taken: Riser bay barricaded to prevent personnel entry
Electronic Technician called to troubleshoot remote console
RGC placed in override mode to level riser joint
Remote console powered down and rebooted
Load holding valves inspected on forward and aft riser crane hooks
Software updated to add 'Yoke Landed' criteria
Job Safety Analysis and work procedure updated to require visual confirmation of hook engagement
event.summary: On April 11, 2017, while picking up riser to begin well 003 in Green Canyon Block 627, the riser gantry crane (RGC) on the Diamond Ocean Blackhornet dropped a joint of riser approximately 19 feet in the riser bay. The RGC operator received a false 'Green Light' signal indicating proper capture when the hooks were not fully engaged. When the remote console was powered down for reboot, the aft crane hook lost hydraulics and released the riser joint, which fell approximately 15 feet and struck additional riser joints before falling an additional 4 feet to the bottom of the riser bay. No personnel were injured due to barricading of the work area.
recommendations: National Oilwell Varco issued product information bulletin for inspection and correction of RGC riser capture device
Adjust position of hydraulic proximity switches to indicate when crane hook locks are fully engaged
Add 'Yoke Landed' criteria to software to ensure full capture of riser joint
Update Job Safety Analysis and work procedures to require visual confirmation that forward and aft hooks are fully engaged prior to lifting

threats.name: Loss of Hydraulic Pressure During Power Cycle
Inadequate Visual Verification of Hook Engagement

controls.name: Riser Bay Barricading
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel access to hazardous work area

supporting_text: The riser bay had been barricaded to prevent personnel from entering the work area, and no personnel were injured as a result of this incident.

controls.name: Load Cell Detection and Software Inhibit
controls.barrier_role: Prevent opening of riser capture devices when load detected

supporting_text: When a load is detected by the load cells, the software inhibits the opening of the riser capture devices.
Once powered down, the aft crane hook lost hydraulics and released the riser joint which allowed it to fall

controls.name: Over-Center Lock Mechanism
controls.barrier_role: Mechanically lock capture fingers during lifts

supporting_text: An over-center lock mechanism mechanically locks the capture fingers during lifts.

It was determined that the riser capture mechanism should not have failed when the system was powered down. The system was designed so that the hydraulics of the system are too weak to open when there is a load on the RGC. The over-center mechanism locks the capture hooks when closed by a link arm which when under a load, exerts force against a mechanical stop/lock.
Once powered down, the aft crane hook lost hydraulics and released the riser joint

controls.name: Weak Hydraulics Design
controls.barrier_role: Prevent opening of capture fingers during lifts due to insufficient hydraulic pressure

supporting_text: The hydraulics are set up to be too weak to open the capture finger during lifts.
Once powered down, the aft crane hook lost hydraulics and released the riser joint

controls.name: Proximity Switch - Green Light Indication
controls.barrier_role: Indicate proper capture of riser joint

supporting_text: The operator received a 'Green Light' on his remote console, which indicated that it had properly secured the riser.
The 'Green Light' on the control panel indicated that the RGC had properly secured the riser joint, but in fact it had not.
The investigation discovered that the proximity switch activated the 'Green Light' without being properly centered and fully secured to the riser.

controls.name: Visual Verification of Hook Engagement
controls.barrier_role: Confirm full engagement of crane hooks before lifting

supporting_text: Lack of Awareness: The deck crew failed to notice that the Riser Gantry Crane hooks were not fully engaged in the locked position.
The Job Safety Analysis (JSA) and work procedure has been updated to require visual conformation that the forward and aft hooks are fully engaged prior to lifting the riser joint.

controls.name: RGC System Shutdown on Unintended Movement
controls.barrier_role: Detect and shut down crane on unintended movement

supporting_text: If the system sees unintended movement within certain values, it will shut down the RGC.
When the operator began to lift the riser joint, the crane stopped moving and began to give a general alarm on the remote console.

controls.name: Remote Console Troubleshooting and Override Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Restore system functionality when console malfunction detected

supporting_text: The RGC operator attempted to level the crane without success, so the operator stopped the operation and called for an Electronic Technician (ET) to troubleshoot the issue.
The ET arrived to find the RGC remote console was not responding and proceeded to place the crane in override mode to level the riser joint.

The ET advised the operator that the remote console had to be powered down, and then powered back up to reboot the system. Once powered down, the aft crane hook lost hydraulics and released the riser joint

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
